compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
**Completion verification — PASS**

All three acceptance criteria are met by the implementation.

---

**AC 1 — Overlays fire only on relevant plans; not-applicable returns no finding (near-zero false-fire)**

MET. The orchestrator (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:184–188`) skips any deterministic overlay whose trigger did not fire. LLM-routed overlays enter the finder but every T-overlay prompt (e.g. `plan_review_T10.md`, `plan_review_T11.md`, `plan_review_T12.md`) opens with "PASS not-applicable" as the baseline. The `applies_at` gating in `registry.applies()` (`registry.py:214–240`) further suppresses overlays outside their declared level/type scope. Eval corpus run (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e4_suite.jsonl`) and the E3 analysis script (`harnesses/analyze_e4.py:97–110`) confirm false-fire measurement is in place and results are recorded.

**AC 2 — Deterministic vs LLM routing per the registry trigger field**

MET. Every T-overlay entry in `criteria_routing.json` carries an `"overlay_routing"` field (`"deterministic"` or `"llm"`). The registry exposes it via `_descriptor_from_prompt` (`registry.py:165`). Deterministic triggers are implemented in `_DET_OVERLAY_RULES` (`registry.py:275–280`) for the low-FP set (T5a, T5d, T7, T12-keyword). The Scope requirement — T10/T11/T12 LLM-routed for plans — is confirmed by `criteria_routing.json` lines 1057, 1100, 1135 (`"overlay_routing": "llm"`) and matches the experimental rationale (E4-confirmed: deterministic keyword over-fires on 'migration'/'deploy' in plan prose).

**AC 3 — Trigger precision measured on the eval corpus**

MET. The E4 harness (`harnesses/e4_singleturn.py`) runs a dedicated TRIGGER job (lines 62–66) comparing `det_overlays()` vs `llm_router()` per ticket and writing `e4_trigger.jsonl`. The analysis script (`analyze_e4.py:97–110`) computes per-overlay det vs LLM fire counts and disagreements. Concrete results are recorded in `docs/experiments/plan-review-finalize-and-threepass.md` (§3): T11 det 2/19 vs LLM 0/19; T12 det 7/19 vs LLM 0/19 — directly confirming the routing decision reflected in the registry.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Triggered overlays T1-T12 ship as library prompts (reviewers/plan_review_T*.md). registry.overlay_triggers fires low-FP DETERMINISTIC set (T5a/T5d/T7/T12); rest LLM-ROUTED via route_criteria; agent-tier overlays one agent each. FP6 anti-FP encoded in T2. Unit-tested. verify-completion: PASS.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
**Completion verification — PASS**

All three acceptance criteria and the Scope requirement are met by the implementation.

---

**AC 1 — Overlays fire only on relevant plans; not-applicable returns no finding (near-zero false-fire)**

MET. `orchestrator.py:209–210` skips any deterministic overlay whose trigger did not fire (`if registry.is_overlay(cid) and cid in triggers and not triggers[cid]: continue`). `registry.py:214–240` (`applies()`) gates overlays by level, ticket type, and suppress_when conditions. Every LLM-routed T-overlay prompt opens with "PASS not-applicable" as the explicit baseline (confirmed in `plan_review_T10.md`, `plan_review_T11.md`, `plan_review_T12.md` line 10 of each). The eval corpus files `e4_suite.jsonl` and `e4_trigger.jsonl` are present in `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/`, and `analyze_e4.py:97–110` implements the false-fire measurement (per-overlay det vs LLM fire counts).

**AC 2 — Deterministic vs LLM routing per the registry trigger field**

MET. `registry.py:165` exposes `"overlay_routing": routing.get("overlay_routing")` in every criterion descriptor. `criteria_routing.json` carries `"overlay_routing": "deterministic"` or `"llm"` on every T-overlay entry (7 deterministic, 7 llm entries found). `registry.py:275–280` defines `_DET_OVERLAY_RULES` for the low-FP deterministic set (T5a, T5d, T7, T12). The Scope requirement — T10/T11/T12 LLM-routed for plans — is confirmed at `criteria_routing.json:1057, 1100, 1135` (`"overlay_routing": "llm"`).

**AC 3 — Trigger precision measured on the eval corpus**

MET. `e4_singleturn.py:62–66` runs a dedicated TRIGGER job comparing `G.det_overlays()` vs `r4.llm_router()` per ticket and writing `e4_trigger.jsonl`. `analyze_e4.py:97–110` computes per-overlay det vs LLM fire counts and disagreements for T10/T11/T12/T8/T9/T1. Concrete results are recorded in `docs/experiments/plan-review-finalize-and-threepass.md` §3 (line 61–63): T11 det 2/19 vs LLM 0 ("both the bash→Python strangler, not data-migration"); T12 det 7/19 vs LLM 0 — directly confirming the routing decision.

**Scope — T1–T12 overlay catalog ships as library prompts**

MET. All 12 T-overlay prompt files (`plan_review_T1.md` through `plan_review_T12.md`, including T5a–T5e sub-variants) are present in `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/`. T1–T12 (all sub-variants) are registered in `CANONICAL_LLM` (`registry.py:86–101`). Unit test `test_overlay_triggers_are_low_fp_set` (`tests/unit/test_plan_review.py:259–261`) exercises the deterministic trigger set.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Overlays must fire only where the plan has surface for them, or the gate over-fires.

## What
- The T1-T12 overlay catalog; deterministic-where-low-FP triggers (T1/T5a/T5d) + an LLM relevance router
  (T8/T6/T9/G6/T10/T11/T12); applies_at level gating.

## Scope
- T10/T11/T12 are LLM-routed for plans (E4-confirmed: deterministic keyword over-fires on rebar's bash->py
  'migration' / ubiquitous 'deploy').

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Overlays fire only on relevant plans; not-applicable returns no finding (near-zero false-fire).
- [ ] Deterministic vs LLM routing per the registry trigger field.
- [ ] Trigger precision measured on the eval corpus.
compiled_state.title: Triggered overlays (deterministic + LLM-routed)
